{
  "claims": [
    "The National Crime Information Center reported 5,712 missing American Indian and Alaska Native women and girls in the U.S.",
    "Only 116 of the 5,712 missing cases were logged by the National Missing and Unidentified Persons System.",
    "California has one of the highest numbers of uninvestigated and unresolved Missing and Murdered Indigenous People cases in the country.",
    "Native American children enter the child welfare system at a rate that is 2.7 times their representation in the population, the highest of any racial group.",
    "National data shows that 85% of all missing Indigenous children over a 10-year period were endangered runaways.",
    "Runaways are especially vulnerable to becoming victims of crime.",
    "Thirteen states and the federal government already grant peace officer status to tribal police."
  ]
}
